failure_surface: Code execution failed with AttributeError when attempting to write to cells while workbook was opened with read_only=True mode
root_cause: Agent initially opened the openpyxl workbook with read_only=True flag, which makes cell value assignments illegal. This is a common mistake when agents assume read-only mode is safe for both reading and subsequent writing operations.
minimal_fix_hypothesis: Always load openpyxl workbooks with read_only=False when any cell modifications will occur. Do not mix read-only loading with write operations on the same workbook instance.
evidence: Initial code used: wb = load_workbook(file_path, read_only=True)
Error traceback shows: AttributeError: 'EmptyCell' object attribute 'value' is read-only
Second attempt fixed this by changing to: wb = load_workbook(file_path, read_only=False, keep_vba=False)
Second attempt executed successfully: Successfully saved results to [output_path]

memory_items.title: Workbook Loading Mode Safety
memory_items.lesson: When modifying spreadsheets with openpyxl, always open the workbook with read_only=False. Read-only mode prevents cell value assignments and causes AttributeError on attempted writes.
memory_items.generalization: Before executing any write operations, verify the workbook is opened in mutable mode. The read_only parameter defaults to True in some scenarios, requiring explicit false value for edit operations.

memory_items.title: Incremental Validation Before Execution
memory_items.lesson: Test basic file operations (open, save) before running complex data processing logic. Simple syntax or configuration errors should be caught early.
memory_items.generalization: Execute minimal test code first to confirm environment setup, file accessibility, and tool compatibility before implementing full solution logic.

memory_items.title: Write Operation Verification
memory_items.lesson: After saving modified workbooks, verify the output file exists and contains the expected changes before confirming success.
memory_items.generalization: Implement post-execution checks: confirm output path validity, validate cell values at target positions, and optionally re-load to confirm persistence.

edits.content:
### Troubleshooting Common Errors
- **AttributeError on cell value assignment**: Ensure workbook is opened with read_only=False. Openpyxl cells are immutable in read-only mode.
- **File save failures**: Verify output directory exists using os.makedirs(), and check write permissions before attempting wb.save().
- **Read-Only Mode Confusion**: Never combine read_only=True loading with subsequent write operations on the same workbook instance.

### Writing Best Practices
- Explicitly set read_only=False when any modifications are planned
- Always wrap save() calls in try-except blocks to catch file system errors
- After saving, re-load the output file in read-only mode to verify changes persisted correctly
